features.name: API Integration Service
features.description: Connects Google Cloud capabilities directly to your application endpoints via standard API pathways.

features.name: Fully Managed Administration
features.description: Abstracts underlying compute and hosting infrastructures, delivering capabilities as serverless resources.

features.name: GCP Platform Governance
features.description: Integrates with central identity, logging, and security systems to enforce governance.

iamRoles.description: Read-only access to browse resources and view basic metrics.
Permissions to modify configuration states and create application resources.

cliExamples.description: Check configuration settings of the local gcloud SDK CLI client
List all active Google Cloud services whitelisted in the project

design: Platform services provide specialized utility, supporting core compute and storage architectures.
Designed to integrate with other services inside your virtual private cloud (VPC) network.
Access capabilities through standard HTTP REST APIs, gcloud CLI commands, or client libraries.
Requires planning resource naming, project hierarchies, and IAM policies before deployment.
Supports standard JSON configuration schemas, ensuring compatibility across custom workloads.
Exports execution logs and diagnostics metrics directly to the Cloud Logging workspace.
Helps decouple application components, promoting modular microservice designs.
Resources are managed under projects, inheriting parent folder and organization policies.
security: Manage service and resource access using Identity and Access Management (IAM) role permissions.
Protect data transfers by enforcing TLS 1.3 transport-level security for all API endpoints.
Supports placing resources inside VPC Service Controls security perimeters to block data exfiltration.
All data at rest is encrypted by default using Google-managed cryptographic keys.
Assign custom service accounts with least privilege roles to run administrative tasks.
Audit admin actions and data transactions by reviewing logs in Cloud Logging.
Use Secret Manager to secure API credentials, keys, and tokens used by the service.
Disable public resource sharing to comply with corporate security standards.
reliability: Serverless execution models scale resources dynamically to handle unpredictable spikes in load.
Workloads are backed by Google Cloud SLAs, guaranteeing high availability and redundancy.
Data is replicated across zones within the region to prevent loss from hardware failures.
Client libraries support automatic retry logic with backoff to bypass transient network errors.
API rate limiters and project quotas prevent control plane exhaustion.
System modifications, failures, and latency indicators are logged to Cloud Logging.
Replicate configurations to Git repository manifests to enable GitOps-based disaster recovery.
Monitor uptime and endpoint health using Cloud Monitoring uptime checks.
cost: Billed on a pay-as-you-go pricing model based on API transactions, storage, or compute usage.
Free tiers are available for developmental usage limits per month.
Configure console budget alerts in Cloud Billing to monitor spending in real-time.
Prune inactive configurations, idle storage blocks, and orphan assets to avoid leak charges.
Deploy instances in regions with lower resource pricing models to reduce monthly costs.
Limit unnecessary API polling rates by using event-driven architectures (Pub/Sub, Eventarc).
Review cost optimization recommendations in Recommender regularly.
Evaluate committed use discounts (CUDs) for predictable, baseline resource workloads.
